pattern: Treating the unflipped cells as a shrinking virtual array and swapping each chosen slot with the last live slot (Fisher-Yates style) samples without replacement in O(1) instead of rejecting repeats.
hints: A set of already-flipped coordinates plus rejection sampling works but degrades badly once most of the matrix is flipped.
Flatten the grid to a single index 0..m*n-1 and imagine an array of 'still available' slots shrinking by one after every flip.
A hash map from logical slot to its current occupant lets you swap the picked slot with the last live slot and shrink the count, without ever allocating the full array.

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 matrix: flip is fully determined, survives resets
1x2 matrix exhausted: both flips must be distinct cells
2x2 matrix flipped to exhaustion then reset and reused
reset called before any flip has happened
narrow 3x1 column exhausted in one pass
large sparse matrix with only a handful of flips (far from exhaustion)

stress.description: small-to-mid matrices with a mix of flip and reset, occasionally driven near exhaustion
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 6)
    n = rng.randint(1, 6)
    total = m * n
    ops = ['Solution']
    argv = [[m, n]]
    flipped_count = 0
    calls = max(1, min(size, 150))
    for _ in range(calls):
        if flipped_count >= total or rng.random() < 0.15:
            ops.append('reset')
            argv.append([])
            flipped_count = 0
        else:
            ops.append('flip')
            argv.append([])
            flipped_count += 1
    return [ops, argv]
